title: Catamaran Charter in Cancun: 2026 Prices and What to Know
html:
Catamaran charter in Cancun offers a stable, spacious way to explore the turquoise waters of the Caribbean Sea. Departing from Marina Puerto Cancun, full-day crewed catamarans typically cost from USD 1,200 for a group of up to twelve guests. Compare verified options on BoatHire24.

Where to set off in Cancun

Cancun has several departure points for catamaran charters. The main hub is Marina Puerto Cancun, a modern complex near the Hotel Zone. Other options include Marina Chac Chi in the Hotel Zone and Isla Mujeres, a short crossing from Punta Sam. You can also set sail from Playa del Carmen, about 70 kilometres south, or from the quieter port at Puerto Morelos. Each offers direct access to the Caribbean Sea and the coral reefs of the Cancun National Marine Park. You can boat hire Cancun for your chosen dates.

Which boat to rent

  1. Bali 4.2 catamaran best for families or two couples, sleeps eight, from USD 1,000 half-day.
  2. Lagoon 52 best for a luxury day charter, up to twelve guests, from USD 1,500 full-day.
  3. Azimut 55 flybridge best for a motor yacht experience, up to ten guests, from USD 2,000 half-day.
  4. Sanyati Leopard 51 best for a spacious cruising catamaran, up to twelve guests, from USD 1,200 full-day. See the Sanyati listing for details.
  5. Sunseeker 50 best for speed and style, up to eight guests, from USD 1,800 half-day.

How much does it cost to rent a catamaran in Cancun?

Prices range from USD 100 per person for a shared tour to over USD 3,000 for a private crewed yacht. The exact cost depends on boat size, duration, and season. Use boat hire Cancun on BoatHire24 to compare verified listings.

  • Shared sunset tour: from USD 100 per person for 2 hours, includes drinks and snorkelling.
  • Small motorboat half-day: from USD 350 for a 20-foot boat, suitable for four guests.
  • Catamaran full-day: from USD 1,200 for a 50-foot vessel with crew, up to twelve guests.
  • Private crewed yacht full-day: from USD 2,500 for a 60-foot luxury yacht, up to twenty guests.

Do you need a licence to rent a catamaran in Cancun?

Licensing requirements depend on the boat size and whether you captain the vessel yourself. For bareboat charters, a valid international boating licence is typically required. For crewed charters, the operator provides the captain and crew, so no personal licence is needed. Always confirm with the operator, as exact requirements can vary. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Cancun or list your own.

When is the best season for catamaran charter in Cancun?

The high season for catamaran charter runs from December to April, when the weather is dry and the north wind, known as 'Nortes', can occasionally bring breezier conditions. The sea is calmest from May to October, though this period includes the rainy season and a higher risk of tropical storms. A local tip:

Book a morning departure during high season to secure calm seas and avoid the afternoon trade winds that can make the return journey choppy.
One option worth a look is the Sanyati listing.

At a glance

  • Price band: USD 100 per person to USD 3,000 per boat
  • Peak months: December to April
  • Trip length: from 2 hours to full day
  • Capacity: up to 20 guests on private yachts
  • Currency: USD (widely accepted)

Questions about boat rental in Cancun

Can I rent a catamaran without a captain in Cancun?

Yes, bareboat catamaran rentals are available for those with a valid licence, but most holidaymakers prefer a crewed charter for convenience.

What is the distance from Cancun to Isla Mujeres by catamaran?

The distance from Cancun's Hotel Zone to Isla Mujeres is about 8 nautical miles, a typical 30-minute sail.

How many days in advance should I book a catamaran charter in Cancun?

For the best selection, book at least two weeks ahead during high season; last-minute bookings are possible in low season.

Are meals included in catamaran charter prices?

Most full-day crewed charters include lunch, snacks, and an open bar, but you should confirm with the operator.

Cancun's catamaran scene offers something for every group, from sunset tours to bespoke excursions. If you are a boat owner, consider listing your vessel on BoatHire24 to reach travellers looking for these experiences.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Cancun and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
